sections.label: THINKING
sections.content: Looking at the image, N+W horizontal is now inside door 1 (bottom-right top). The level isn't completed yet. Let me check the current state of all N squares.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
seg = current_frame.segmentation['nodes']
print("Current N squares:")
for n in seg:
    if n['color'] == 'N':
        center_color = next(x['color'] for x in seg if x['id'] in n['children'])
        print(f"  boundary={n['boundary']} center={center_color} pixels={n['pixels']}")

# Check door 1 area
ascii_lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
print("\nDoor 1 area:")
for r in range(37, 44):
    print(f"{r:3d}: {ascii_lines[r][50:62]}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
N+W horizontal is now inside door 1 at rows 39-41, cols 54-59. The level isn't completed yet. I need to move the other agents too.
But wait, I can only control N+W. How do I control the other agents? Maybe I need to click on them to switch control?
Let me try clicking on one of the other N squares to see if it becomes the controllable agent.
Actually, looking at the pattern from before:
- Clicking on an N square swaps its center color with the current N+W agent
- The N+W agent is always the controllable one
So maybe the strategy is:
1. Move N+W to a door
2. Click on another N square to swap centers (making that agent N+W)
3. Move the new N+W to its door
4. Repeat until all agents are in their doors
But the problem is that clicking swaps centers, so the agent that was N+W becomes something else. Let me think about this more carefully.
Current state:
- N+W horizontal: in door 1 (rows 39-41, cols 54-59) - already placed!
- N+c vertical: rows 42-47, cols 33-35 (6x3) - needs to go to top-left door (6x3)
- N+B square: rows 45-50, cols 42-47 (6x6) - needs to go to bottom-left door (6x6)
- N+c bottom: rows 54-56, cols 51-53 (3x3) - needs to go to door 2 (3x3)
If I click on N+c vertical, it becomes N+W and the current N+W (horizontal, in door 1) becomes N+c. But I don't want to disturb the agent that's already in door 1!
Maybe the goal is to place all agents in their doors, and the center colors don't matter? Or maybe the center colors need to match the door interiors (all white)?
Let me try clicking on N+c vertical to make it N+W, then move it to the top-left door.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Current state:
# N+W horizontal: in door 1 (rows 39-41, cols 54-59) - placed
# N+c vertical: rows 42-47, cols 33-35 (6x3) - needs top-left door (6x3)
# N+B square: rows 45-50, cols 42-47 (6x6) - needs bottom-left door (6x6)
# N+c bottom: rows 54-56, cols 51-53 (3x3) - needs door 2 (3x3)

# Click on N+c vertical to make it N+W
# This will swap centers: N+c vertical becomes N+W, N+W horizontal becomes N+c
predict(expect_change=True, note='Click N+c vertical to make it N+W')
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 44, 'col': 34}])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
